name: Goodyear Tire v. Haeger
question: When a court does not afford the sanctioned party the additional procedural protections required to impose punitive sanctions, is the court required to tailor the amount of the sanction to the harm directly caused by the sanctionable conduct, as is required for the imposition of compensatory sanctions?
description: A case in which the court held that, when a federal court sanctions bad-faith conduct by ordering a litigant to pay the other side’s legal fees, the award is limited to the fees the innocent party incurred solely due to the misconduct.
